summary: Iko Iko opener, fiery Shakedown>Playing jam, strong second‑set flow
blurb: The show kicks off with a thunderous Iko Iko and delivers a high‑energy second set highlighted by the Shakedown Street → Playing in the Band segue and a heartfelt Dear Mr. Fantasy. Jerry’s vocals and guitar soar while Brent adds moving keyboards; the encore closes with a solid U.S. Blues.
review:
June 14, 1984 at Red Rocks found the Dead firing on all cylinders. A monster Iko Iko launches the night, showcasing Garcia’s lung‑shredding vocal power and soaring lead lines that set an intense tone for the evening. The first set wanders a bit—Cassidy and New Minglewood Blues feel serviceable, but Jack Straw and Keep Your Day Job keep things moving forward.  
The second set is where the magic truly ignites: Shakedown Street rockets straight into a blistering Playing in the Band jam, then flows into an inspired Dear Mr. Fantasy where Brent’s strained yet moving vocal adds extra poignancy. The Drums‑Space‑Playing sequence rides a tight groove, and the band rolls through Black Peter, Throwing Stones and a rousing Not Fade Away before bowing out with a classic U.S. Blues encore.  
Across the board recordings, Garcia’s guitar work remains a highlight, Phil’s bass underpins the whole thing even when his presence is muted in some mixes, Bob provides solid rhythm‑guitar texture and occasional vocal flair, Brent shines on organ during Black Peter and adds heartfelt vocals on Fantasy, while Mickey’s space‑time xylophone‑like textures and Billy’s steady drumming keep the rhythmic engine humming. The soundboard captures are crisp and well‑balanced, making this a must‑listen show for anyone wanting a vivid snapshot of the 1984 Dead at Red Rocks.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive (highlighting Iko Iko, Dear Mr. Fantasy and strong jams) while the remaining four are mixed but contain no notable negative criticism. The overall tone leans modestly positive, warranting a moderate sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, well‑balanced soundboard capture with minimal technical flaws; it delivers the clearest representation of the band’s high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Legendary Iko Iko opener that sets an intense tone
Fiery Shakedown Street → Playing in the Band segue and jam
Heartfelt Dear Mr. Fantasy with Brent’s moving vocal
Second‑set flow culminating in Black Peter, Throwing Stones and a solid U.S. Blues encore

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers lung‑shredding vocals and soaring guitar solos, especially on Iko Iko, Jack Straw and the Shakedown/Playing jams.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation throughout; his parts are occasionally masked in some board mixes but remain musically tight.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm work is steady; his vocals fizzle during Playing but shine on U.S. Blues, and he adds slide‑style flair on New Minglewood Blues.
band_performance.Brent: Shows a strained yet moving vocal on Dear Mr. Fantasy and supplies rich organ textures that lift Black Peter and the second‑set flow.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums and space segment are highlighted by ethnic xylophone‑like sounds, adding color to the improvisational sections.
band_performance.Billy: Keeps a solid, propulsive beat that underpins the high‑energy jams, especially during the Shakedown Street → Playing transition.
